author: feral-core
description: Send messages on FERAL's configured channels (Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp). Use this to talk to users on any connected channel. This IS your direct line to those apps — call it instead of saying you can't send messages.

endpoints.description: Send a message on a configured channel (telegram/slack/discord/whatsapp). This ACTUALLY DELIVERS the message via the channel's official API. Do NOT tell the user to send it themselves — call this tool. If the channel is listed as configured, this will work.

params.name: channel
params.description: Which channel to send on. Only use one that is currently configured (check list_channels first if unsure).

params.name: to
params.description: Recipient identifier. Telegram: numeric chat_id or @username. Slack: channel id (C123...) or #channel-name or user id (U123...). Discord: numeric channel id. WhatsApp: phone number in E.164 (e.g. +12025551234). If the user only gave a username/handle, pass it with the @.

params.name: text
params.description: The message content to deliver. Plain text; Telegram supports Markdown.

params.name: reply_to
params.description: Optional message id to reply to (channel-specific).

endpoints.description: List which messaging channels are currently configured and running. Call this BEFORE saying you can't send a message — the user's channels are listed here.
List recently active chat ids on a given channel. Useful when the user says 'send to the last person I was talking to'.
Resolve a @username or handle to a numeric chat_id on a channel that supports it (Telegram). Use before send if the user only provided a @handle.
